signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD exhibits a 1.274% G8 spread as NZD outperforms all peers while CAD remains the weekly laggard. NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) diverges from the RBNZ hike bias, creating short-squeeze risk if the May 27 meeting confirms hawkishness. CAD remains pressured by a - vs USD and a -0.640% average weekly decline across G8 pairs. NZD/CAD strength persists as long as RBNZ hike expectations remain intact.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.84 remains supported by a +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage despite Middle East tensions and potential Strait of Hormuz disruptions. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) represents a crowded carry trade vulnerable to volatility, but safe-haven JPY demand remains marginal with VIX at 17.2 (normal volatility). Carry dominates the regime until VIX exceeds 25 or the 160.00 intervention level is tested.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.722 vs -0.125 norm (z=+1.78σ) indicates a significant regime shift where EUR is tracking equity sentiment rather than traditional macro drivers. EUR COT LF net short -18,293 (45.4% long) shows specs remain cautious despite the 1M +2.06% price appreciation. The +175bp USD carry advantage structurally limits upside ahead of the June ECB meeting. Watch for NFP data to confirm if USD funding stress returns.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7227 faces a positioning extreme with COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) while retail sentiment is 84% short. This institutional vs retail divergence at the upper end of the monthly range (+4.96%) suggests retail is unsuccessfully fading the trend. The +60bp AUD carry premium vs USD supports the bid, but high spec length introduces long-unwind risk if NFP surprises to the upside. Watch 0.7250 for retail capitulation.
